target.name: Lightelligence
target.founder: Shen Yichen, Marin Soljačić, Huaiyu Meng, Spencer Powers

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.1B - $39.2B.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $39.2B

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $2.26B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.09B

data.narrative:
Lightelligence is a public-stage semiconductor company that has raised $310M+. The valuation analysis relies on a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage public comparables: Cerebras ($39.2B), Ouster ($2.3B), and Aeva Technologies ($1.1B).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given data availability. The median valuation across these three public semiconductor peers is $2.3B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $39.2B. This wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in market cap outcomes within the public semiconductor cohort, driven by differences in revenue scale, profitability, and investor sentiment toward different subsegments of the sector.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.9B to $2.6B, centered on the $2.3B peer median. Key uncertainties include Lightelligence's differentiated position relative to the comparables on revenue traction, path to profitability, and capital efficiency relative to capital raised. The broad range in the peer set ($1.1B–$39.2B) also signals that single-method triangulation carries elevated sensitivity to peer selection. A more robust estimate would benefit from revenue multiples, growth rates, or discounted cash flow analysis if such data becomes available.
